nodes.name: 红嘴山鸦
nodes.summary: 红嘴山鸦（Red-billed chough），在图谱中标记为易危（VU）物种，主要分布于阿尔卑斯山、地中海、中亚，主要威胁包括人类活动扰动、气候变化。

nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。

nodes.name: 人类活动扰动
nodes.summary: 旅游、施工和高频活动影响繁殖或觅食。

nodes.name: 阿尔卑斯山
nodes.summary: 阿尔卑斯山是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括小嘴鸻、鵰鴞、黄嘴山鸦等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 地中海
nodes.summary: 地中海是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括大红鹳、小凤头燕鸥、細嘴鷗等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 中亚
nodes.summary: 中亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联34种鸟类，包括斑头雁、大鸨、小鸨等34种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The red-billed chough breeds in Ireland, western Great Britain, the Isle of Man, Brittany, the Alps, La Palma in the Canary Islands, across southern Europe and the Mediterranean basin, northern Arabia, northern Iraq, Iran, Afghanistan, and in mountainous country across Central Asia, India and China, with two separate populations in the Ethiopian Highlands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The red-billed chough breeds in Ireland, western Great Britain, the Isle of Man, Brittany, the Alps, La Palma in the Canary Islands, across southern Europe and the Mediterranean basin, northern Arabia, northern Iraq, Iran, Afghanistan, and in mountainous country across Central Asia, India and China, with two separate populations in the Ethiopian Highlands

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: The red-billed chough breeds in Ireland, western Great Britain, the Isle of Man, Brittany, the Alps, La Palma in the Canary Islands, across southern Europe and the Mediterranean basin, northern Arabia, northern Iraq, Iran, Afghanistan, and in mountainous country across Central Asia, India and China, with two separate populations in the Ethiopian Highlands

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Over its range as a whole, the species is not believed to approach the thresholds for the global population decline criterion of the IUCN Red List (i.e., declining more than 30% in ten years or three generations), and is therefore evaluated as least concern. However, the European range has declined and fragmented due to the loss of traditional pastoral farming, persecution and perhaps disturbance at breeding and nesting sites, although the numbers in France, Great Britain and Ireland may now have stabilised. The European breeding population is between 12,265 and 17,370 pairs, but only in Spain is the species still widespread

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The local extinction of the Alpine chough and the reduced range of red-billed chough in the islands may have been due to climate change or human activity. A small group of wild red-billed chough arrived naturally in Cornwall in 2001, and nested in the following year
